items.title: Feature selection before deployment
items.description: Teams audit hundreds of candidate columns and drop low-signal dimensions to avoid sparsity-driven overfitting — the curse explains why exhaustive feature dumps hurt distance and tree baselines alike.

items.title: PCA and embedding pipelines
items.description: Genomics, text, and vision workflows project high-d raw inputs to 10–200 components before clustering or k-NN retrieval because sample complexity in full space is prohibitive.

items.title: Anomaly detection threshold tuning
items.description: Distance-to-k-th-neighbor scores become noisy in high d; security teams reduce dimension or switch models when concentration makes all transactions look equally 'normal' by distance.

items.title: Experimental design for surveys
items.description: Market researchers limit question batteries because each added Likert dimension multiplies the sample size needed to observe stable local segments in response space.

items.title: Recommender cold-start analysis
items.description: User taste vectors with thousands of sparse item dimensions suffer hubness and weak neighbor overlap — motivates matrix factorization and dimensionality reduction instead of raw k-NN on full catalogs.
